---
title: "Home"
---

::: {.hero}
# Li Tang

## Bioinformatics Researcher & Educator

Turning biological questions into clear bioinformatics workflows.

::: {.button-row}
[Research](research.qmd){.custom-button}
[Tutorials](tutorials.qmd){.custom-button}
[Contact](contact.qmd){.custom-button}
:::
:::

## About Me

I am a bioinformatics postdoctoral researcher working on mitochondrial genomics, variant interpretation, and sequencing data analysis.

I create practical bioinformatics content for experimental scientists, PhD students, and postdocs who want to better understand data analysis.

## Featured Video

::: {.video-wrapper}
<iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/VIDEO_ID"
title="Featured YouTube video"
frameborder="0"
allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::

[Watch on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID){.custom-button}

## What I Do

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.custom-card}
### Research

Mitochondrial genomics, variant interpretation, NUMTs, heteroplasmy, and comparative genomics.
:::

::: {.custom-card}
### Tutorials

Practical bioinformatics tutorials for experimental scientists, PhD students, and postdocs.
:::

::: {.custom-card}
### Resources

Checklists, templates, code snippets, and workflow notes for sequencing data analysis.
:::

:::

## Research Focus

- Mitochondrial DNA variation
- Primate comparative genomics
- NUMTs and heteroplasmy artifacts
- Large-scale WGS/WES analysis
- Variant interpretation
